definition: Supply elasticity varies enormously across commodity classes and is the decisive analytical variable for evaluating supercycle claims. Shale oil (~0.5-1.0) can respond in months, lithium (~0.3-0.5) in 2-4 years, wheat (~0.5-0.7) in 6-18 months, while copper (~0.05) requires 12-18 years for greenfield supply response.
evidence: Greenfield copper mine timelines extended from 5.8yr median (1990s) to 12.5yr (2010s)
Quantified elasticity ranking across major commodities
All four debates confirmed this as the binding analytical variable
Brownfield elasticity remains 0.15-0.25 providing partial offset
open_questions: Can technological innovation (AI-guided exploration, autonomous mining) compress greenfield timelines?
How does brownfield vs. greenfield elasticity shift as ore grades decline?
